SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.io

Class Payload

java.lang.Object
com.dua3.utility.io.Payload
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public final class Payload extends Object implements AutoCloseable
Represents a payload abstraction for handling resources identified by a URI.

This class supports both file-based and stream-based resources, providing high-performance zero-copy optimizations for files and fallback mechanisms for streams. It also extracts "magic bytes" to identify the type or format of the payload content. The class is immutable and supports lazy initialization for its resource handling components.

The Payload instances can be created via a factory method: - fromUri(URI): Determines the resource type based on the URI scheme and creates the payload.

  • Method Details

    • fromUri

      public static Payload fromUri(URI uri) throws IOException
      Factory method to create a Payload instance from a given URI. This method determines the type of the resource (local file, remote stream, etc.) based on the URI scheme and delegates to the appropriate factory method.
      Parameters:
      uri - the URI of the resource to create the payload from. If the scheme is "file" or null, the resource is treated as a local file. For other schemes (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, JAR), the resource is treated as a stream.
      Returns:
      a Payload instance representing the resource.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing or reading the resource.

    • fromPath

      public static Payload fromPath(Path path) throws IOException
      Creates a Payload instance from the provided Path. This method is a factory for creating payloads from local file paths. It determines the resource's magic bytes and utilizes zero-copy techniques for efficient resource access.
      Parameters:
      path - the Path representing the local file to create the Payload from. The file must exist and be readable.
      Returns:
      a Payload instance representing the resource located at the specified path.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing or reading the file.

    • fromFile

      public static Payload fromFile(File file) throws IOException
      Creates a Payload instance from the provided Path. This method is a factory for creating payloads from local file paths. It determines the resource's magic bytes and utilizes zero-copy techniques for efficient resource access.
      Parameters:
      file - the File representing the local file to create the Payload from. The file must exist and be readable.
      Returns:
      a Payload instance representing the resource located at the specified path.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing or reading the file.

    • fromUrl

      public static Payload fromUrl(URL url) throws IOException
      Creates a Payload instance from the provided Path. This method is a factory for creating payloads from local file paths. It determines the resource's magic bytes and utilizes zero-copy techniques for efficient resource access.
      Parameters:
      url - the URL to create the Payload from.
      Returns:
      a Payload instance representing the resource located at the specified path.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing or reading the file.

    • fromInputStream

      public static Payload fromInputStream(InputStream in) throws IOException
      Creates a Payload instance from the provided InputStream. This method serves as a factory to build a Payload object containing the data from the input stream.
      Parameters:
      in - the InputStream to create the Payload object from. The input stream is consumed and wrapped inside the Payload.
      Returns:
      a Payload instance representing the resource encapsulated in the stream.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading or processing the input stream.

    • uri

      public Optional<URI> uri()
      Retrieves the URI associated with this payload instance.
      Returns:
      the URI representing the resource associated with the payload.

    • magic8Bytes

      public long magic8Bytes()
      Retrieves the magic bytes associated with this payload instance. This method returns a long value representing a signature or identifier typically used for type or format identification of the payload.
      Returns:
      the long value representing the magic bytes of the payload.

    • stream

      public InputStream stream() throws IOException
      Provides an InputStream for the current instance.

      Note: Calling this method consumes the payload. any attempt to access the payload again will result in an exception.

      Returns:
      an InputStream representing the resource associated with this payload.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating or retrieving the stream.
      IllegalStateException - if the payload has already been consumed.

    • channel

      public ReadableByteChannel channel() throws IOException
      Provides a ReadableByteChannel for accessing the content of the payload.

      Note: Calling this method consumes the payload. any attempt to access the payload again will result in an exception.

      Returns:
      a ReadableByteChannel instance for reading the payload content.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during channel creation or access.
      IllegalStateException - if the payload has already been consumed.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Closes the resources associated with this payload instance.

      This method ensures that both the channel and the protectedStream are properly closed, releasing any resources associated with them.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while closing the resources.
